Sinotrans ( (HK:0598) ) has provided an update.

Sinotrans Limited has scheduled a board meeting for 28 April 2026 to review and approve the unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended 31 March 2026. The board will also consider the publication of these quarterly figures, a step that will provide investors and other stakeholders with an early indication of the company’s operating performance and business momentum in 2026.

The announcement, issued from Beijing and signed by company secretary Li Shichu, also confirms the current composition of the board, including executive and independent non-executive directors. The forthcoming disclosure of first-quarter results will be closely watched by the market as a gauge of trading conditions in the logistics sector and Sinotrans’ positioning amid evolving freight and supply-chain dynamics.

More about Sinotrans

Sinotrans Limited is a mainland China-based logistics services provider listed in Hong Kong, offering integrated freight forwarding, shipping agency and related logistics solutions. The company operates through a network of subsidiaries, serving domestic and international trade flows and supporting clients’ supply chain and transportation needs across key markets.

data.description:

CROSSTEC Group Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:3893) ) has issued an announcement.

CROSSTEC Group Holdings reported the conversion of a portion of its outstanding convertible bonds held by MKI into equity. MKI converted HK$6.6 million in principal at a conversion price of HK$0.11 per share, resulting in the issuance of 60 million new shares on April 14, 2026.

Following the conversion, CROSSTEC’s issued share capital increased from 546,624,000 to 606,624,000 shares, diluting existing holdings but strengthening MKI’s position as the largest shareholder. MKI now owns 174,700,000 shares, or about 28.8% of the enlarged share capital, while public float has declined in percentage terms though the absolute number of public shares remains unchanged.

More about CROSSTEC Group Holdings Ltd.

An update from United Co RUSAL ( (HK:0486) ) is now available.

United Company RUSAL has disclosed that SUAL Partners International LLC has terminated its direct right to dispose of a certain number of votes attached to RUSAL’s voting shares. The change stems from SUAL Partners’ acquisition of a participation share in the issuer, altering its status as a direct holder of voting rights and updating the company’s shareholder control profile for the Moscow Exchange and investors.

The notice, filed as an overseas regulatory announcement in Hong Kong and in Russia, formalizes the shift in voting rights structure without indicating any joint disposal arrangements or indirect control chains. While the operational impact is not detailed, the disclosure clarifies governance and ownership transparency, which is material for stakeholders tracking control dynamics and regulatory compliance across RUSAL’s multiple listings.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

United Co RUSAL ( (HK:0486) ) has issued an announcement.

United Company RUSAL has disclosed that SUAL Partners International Limited Liability Company no longer has the right of direct, independent disposal over a certain number of votes attached to RUSAL’s voting shares. The notice, filed with the Moscow Exchange and mirrored on the company’s disclosure platforms, indicates that no significant indirect or joint voting rights remain that meet the 5% reporting threshold.

The change affects SUAL Partners’ governance influence over RUSAL but does not specify the exact size of the former stake, beyond confirming that post-termination holdings do not trigger additional disclosure chains. Investors may view this as a shift in the company’s shareholder structure and potential rebalancing of control among major stakeholders, although RUSAL has not announced any immediate operational or strategic consequences linked to this development.

More about United Co RUSAL

United Company RUSAL is an international public joint-stock company incorporated in Jersey and continued in the Russian Federation, with shares listed in Hong Kong and on Russian exchanges. The group operates in the aluminium industry, producing primary aluminium and related products for global industrial and manufacturing customers, and is a key player in Russia’s metals sector and international commodity markets.

data.title: Prosper Construction Formalizes Terms for Board Risk Management Committee
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Prosper Construction Holdings Limited ( (HK:6816) ) has shared an announcement.

Prosper Construction Holdings Limited has formally set out the terms of reference for its Board Risk Management Committee, which was established by resolution on 22 June 2016. The committee must consist of at least three directors, with a majority being independent non-executive directors, and is chaired and appointed by the board, with the company secretary serving as its secretary.

The document details procedures for convening meetings, notice periods, quorum requirements, and the frequency of at least one meeting per year, with flexibility for additional sessions as needed. It also clarifies that written resolutions signed by all committee members carry the same weight as in-person decisions, and members are not permitted to appoint alternates, underscoring a focus on accountability and robust risk oversight in the company’s governance framework.

More about Prosper Construction Holdings Limited

Prosper Construction Holdings Limited operates in the construction industry, providing building and related construction services. The company is governed by a board of directors that includes a mix of executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting standard corporate governance practices for listed construction firms.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

An update from Prosper Construction Holdings Limited ( (HK:6816) ) is now available.

Prosper Construction Holdings Limited has formalised detailed terms of reference for its remuneration committee, setting out its constitution, membership requirements and operating procedures. The committee must comprise at least three directors with a majority of independent non-executive directors, be chaired by an independent non-executive director, and meet at least annually.

The rules emphasize that remuneration should be sufficient to attract and retain directors without being excessive, and explicitly bar directors from voting on their own pay. By codifying notice periods, quorum rules, meeting formats and written resolution mechanisms, the company strengthens governance and transparency around directors’ pay-setting, which may enhance investor confidence and align with regulatory best practices in Hong Kong’s capital markets.

data.title: Prosper Construction Formalizes Audit Committee Mandate to Strengthen Governance
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Prosper Construction Holdings Limited ( (HK:6816) ) has provided an announcement.

Prosper Construction Holdings Limited has set out formal terms of reference for its Audit Committee, originally established by a board resolution on 22 June 2016, detailing how members are appointed, removed, and structured to align with Hong Kong Listing Rules. The document specifies that the committee must comprise at least three non-executive directors, with a majority being independent and at least one possessing professional accounting or financial expertise, while also barring recent partners of the current auditing firm and formalizing procedures for convening meetings, notices and secretarial support to strengthen corporate governance.

Under the updated framework, the Audit Committee chairman must be an independent non-executive director appointed by the board or elected by committee members, and the company secretary will serve as the committee’s secretary unless a substitute is chosen for a specific meeting. These measures are designed to reinforce the independence, transparency and effectiveness of Prosper Construction’s financial oversight and audit processes, which may enhance investor confidence and compliance with regulatory standards.

More about Prosper Construction Holdings Limited

Prosper Construction Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company engaged in the construction sector. The group operates through a board comprising executive and non-executive directors, including independent non-executive directors, and is subject to the corporate governance and listing requirements of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

data.title: Ab&B Bio-Tech Auditor Resigns Amid Probe Into Fund Investments and Consultancy Payments
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ab&B Bio-Tech CO., LTD. JS Class H ( (HK:2627) ) just unveiled an update.

Ab&B Bio-Tech announced that Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu has resigned as its auditor with immediate effect amid an ongoing delay in publishing the company’s 2025 annual results and a suspension of share trading in Hong Kong. Deloitte cited grave concerns over the commercial substance and business rationale of approximately HK$272 million in fund investments and about HK$58 million in consultancy payments linked to entities with common directorships, and said the company’s plan to appoint a new auditor made their professional relationship untenable.

The board and its audit committee said they take the issues seriously and have engaged independent professional advisers to conduct a phased review of the fund investments and consultancy payments, requiring management to cooperate fully. The company plans to identify and appoint a new auditor as soon as practicable, subject to shareholder approval at a general meeting, and has pledged to update investors as further developments arise, underscoring continued uncertainty over its financial reporting and governance processes.

More about Ab&B Bio-Tech CO., LTD. JS Class H

Ab&B Bio-Tech CO., LTD. JS is a biotechnology company based in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong. The group focuses on bio-tech related operations, and its shares have been suspended from trading following delays in publishing its 2025 annual results, placing it under heightened regulatory and investor scrutiny.

data.title: Suzhou Ribo Advances Lead siRNA Anticoagulant With EMA Phase 2b Filing
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Suzhou Ribo Life Science Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6938) ) has provided an announcement.

Suzhou Ribo Life Science has submitted a phase 2b clinical trial application to the European Medicines Agency for its core siRNA drug Vortosiran, aimed at stroke prevention in patients with atrial fibrillation. The therapy is positioned as the world’s first siRNA factor XI inhibitor to reach clinical trials in atrial fibrillation, advancing the company’s strategy in innovative anticoagulation treatments.

Vortosiran is designed to selectively and durably suppress factor XI to block FXI-mediated coagulation and potentially reduce bleeding risk compared with existing anticoagulants, targeting a key unmet need in thromboembolic protection. The candidate is currently the most advanced siRNA-based FXI inhibitor in clinical development, having completed a phase 2a study in coronary artery disease in Europe, although the company cautions that successful development and commercialization are not assured, underscoring ongoing clinical and regulatory risk for investors and partners.

More about Suzhou Ribo Life Science Co., Ltd. Class H

Suzhou Ribo Life Science Co., Ltd. is a China-based biopharmaceutical company focused on RNA interference technologies, developing small interfering RNA (siRNA) therapies for thrombotic and cardiovascular diseases. Its core pipeline targets coagulation pathways, aiming to offer next-generation anticoagulant options with improved safety and efficacy profiles for patients at risk of thromboembolic events.

Beijing Enterprises Holdings ( (HK:0392) ) has issued an announcement.

Fujian Yanjing Huiquan Brewery, a Shanghai-listed subsidiary of Beijing Enterprises Holdings, reported audited 2025 results showing revenue of RMB 640.3 million, down 1.05% year on year, but a 23.14% rise in net profit to RMB 80.1 million as profitability improved. The brewer proposed a cash dividend of RMB 1.00 per 10 shares, signalling confidence in its earnings quality and returning more cash to shareholders. It also released its 2025 annual report, audit report, ESG and CSR disclosures, and announced changes to governance, including board resolutions, amendments to its articles of association, a notice of its 2025 AGM, and the resignation of its chairman alongside the election of a new director, pointing to an ongoing refresh of its corporate structure and oversight.

data.title: AEON Stores Seeks Full Control of AEON GD via RMB170 Million Tender Bid
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from AEON Stores (Hong Kong) Co., Limited ( (HK:0984) ).

AEON Stores (Hong Kong) Co., Limited plans to bid for the remaining 35% equity interest in its non-wholly owned subsidiary AEON GD, currently held by Teemall Department Stores, through a public tender at a maximum consideration of about RMB170.27 million. The move would turn AEON GD into a wholly owned subsidiary and is classified as a very substantial acquisition under Hong Kong listing rules.

The board argues that participating directly in the tender will prevent delays in the disposal of the stake and allow faster execution of profitability enhancement plans for AEON GD. As the tender documentation cannot be made conditional on prior shareholder approval, the company is seeking an advance mandate at an extraordinary general meeting, while the connected nature of the deal triggers reporting and announcement obligations but is exempt from circular and independent advice requirements.

More about AEON Stores (Hong Kong) Co., Limited

AEON Stores (Hong Kong) Co., Limited operates retail and department store businesses, with a focus on general merchandise and consumer goods in Hong Kong and mainland China. The company holds a controlling 65% stake in its non-wholly owned subsidiary AEON GD, which is engaged in retail operations alongside minority shareholder Teemall Department Stores holding the remaining 35%.
